reason_for_decision: The article has a usable foundation, but it does not yet meet the strict pass threshold because it is too short and needs deeper treatment of architecture, latency, and operator workflow.
major_issues: The article is below the strict 1000-word pass floor.
It needs more concrete treatment of end-to-end latency budgets, degraded modes, and how operators close events in real-time systems.
The Cyrentis-specific section weakens editorial neutrality relative to the rest of the reviewed knowledge base.
revision_actions: Expand the article to exceed 1000 words with more depth on data flow, buffering, failover, and monitoring-system metrics.
Replace the product-specific section with a more neutral architecture discussion of platform layers and operator closure.
Strengthen the distinction between live feeds, actionable real-time workflows, and resilient event handling.
strengths: The article already starts from latency budget rather than a vague live-feed definition.
Its alert-discipline and resilience framing is directionally correct.
The connection to situational awareness is a good starting point for a fuller article.
summary_assessment: This article has the right overall direction, but it needs expansion and a more neutral architecture-level treatment before it can pass the stricter workflow.
